facts.text: Glen Ridge was officially incorporated on December 11, 1947, after resident Helen Mosler suggested the name because the wooded canal bank looked like a glen ridge.
The town is a registered bird sanctuary, where dense green foliage creates a canopy that drapes over the streets as you drive in.
In 1932, the Trout family built and moved into the first house that would become the core of what is now Glen Ridge.
The original housing development split in 1951, with part of it evolving into the neighboring town of Cloud Lake.
Glen Ridge opened a permanent town hall and community center in September 2002 after spending about $110,000 to repurpose a former residence.
Twin brothers Kenyon and Karl Riddle bought land west of the Seaboard Air Line Railroad in 1935, laying the groundwork for the town.
